content.problemStatement:
**Short Answer - 2.5 marks**

Given an integer array numsnumsnums, find the **subarray** with the largest sum, and print its sum.

**Note**: A subarray is a contiguous non-empty sequence of elements within an array.


Input Format

The first line contains TTT, the number of test cases.
The first line in each test case contains NNN, the number of elements in an array.
The second line in each test case contains NNN integers, denoting the elements in the array.


Output Format
For each test case, output the maximum subarray sum of each array.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case, the first line contains an integer N, the size of the array. The second line contains N space‑separated integers representing the array elements.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a single line containing the maximum possible sum of any non‑empty contiguous subarray of the given array.

sampleTestCases.explanation: First array: maximum subarray is [4, -1, 2, 1] with sum 6. Second array: only element 1, sum 1. Third array: subarray [5, 4, -1, 7, 8] gives sum 23.

solution.approach: For each test case we scan the array once while maintaining two variables: • current_sum – the maximum sum of a subarray that ends at the current position. It is updated as current_sum = max(num, current_sum + num). • max_sum – the best sum seen so far over all positions, updated as max_sum = max(max_sum, current_sum). This is Kadane's algorithm and runs in linear time. The algorithm works for all sign combinations because it always considers starting a new subarray at the current element if extending the previous one would be worse. The answer is stored in max_sum after the scan.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, i.e., O(T·N) overall.
